lawName: Child and Adolescent Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act, 1986
overview: Prohibits employment of children in all occupations and regulates employment of adolescents in accordance with the Act and applicable rules.

definitions.definition: A person who has not completed fourteen years of age.
A person who has completed fourteen years but has not completed eighteen years.
Employer responsible for compliance under the Act.

rules.title: Prohibit Employment of Children
rules.description: Ensure no child is employed in violation of the provisions of the Act.

citations.reference: Child and Adolescent Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act, 1986

rules.title: Review Adolescent Employment
rules.description: Ensure adolescent employment complies with statutory restrictions and applicable conditions.

rules.title: Maintain Age Verification Records
rules.description: Maintain records supporting age verification of employees wherever required.

rules.title: Display Statutory Notices
rules.description: Display notices where required under applicable statutory provisions.

exceptions.title: Statutory Exceptions
exceptions.description: Employment of adolescents is subject to statutory restrictions, prohibited occupations and prescribed conditions.
